body:
## What changed

- Extended the static asset manifest reference extractor to include local `poster`, `srcset`, and `imagesrcset` references.
- Added fixtures for responsive image candidates, preload image candidates, video posters, and ignored external/data/hash/mailto/tel references.
- Restored the static asset test command so it checks the committed manifest before running reference tests.
- Refreshed the stale `ebook.js` fingerprint in `site/asset-manifest.json`.

## Why

The static-site manifest gate previously only checked scalar `src` and `href` attributes. Responsive image and media references could be loaded by the browser without being represented in the committed asset manifest. The package test command also did not run the freshness check expected by the live-site asset-manifest test.

## Validation

- `npm run test:static-site-asset-references`
- `node --test scripts/check-live-site.test.js --test-name-pattern "asset manifest"`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run lint`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run test:ownership`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run format:check -- scripts/update-site-asset-manifest.js scripts/static-site-asset-references.test.js package.json`
- `node --check scripts/update-site-asset-manifest.js && node --check scripts/static-site-asset-references.test.js && node --check scripts/check-live-site.test.js`
- `git diff --check`
title: [codex] Cover static media asset references
